name: Turkey Soup from Loren Martin
recipeIngredient: 1 Meaty turkey carcass, leftover from a 20 pound bird
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the meat from the turkey carcass after dinner and place the carcass in a large pot.
2. Add all of the spices, water or broth, and optionally a cup or two of white wine.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let simmer for 6-8 additional hours.
4. Allow the soup to cool, strain it through a fine mesh sieve, and discard any fat that rises to the surface.
5. Return the strained broth to the pot and add large pieces of cooked turkey as well as coarsely chopped fresh vegetables like celery or carrots.
6. Add wild rice, barley, or other legumes and simmer (covered) until the grains are fully cooked and the vegetables are crisp-tender.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve warm. This recipe also freezes well for later use.
